Unnamed: 2: Annual India Tourism Statistics (ITS) 2015–2024; data.gov.in portal; tourism.gov.in — provides state-wise and monument-wise domestic & foreign tourist visits, receipts, and employment statistics.
Monument-wise visitor data for centrally protected monuments (Taj Mahal, Ajanta, Ellora, Khajuraho, Hampi, Sanchi, Bodh Gaya, etc.) — publicly released each year.
Official reports from UP Tourism, Tamil Nadu Tourism, Karnataka Tourism, Rajasthan Tourism, Kerala Tourism, Goa Tourism, Himachal Tourism, etc. State Annual Reports 2015–2025.
data.gov.in datasets on tourist arrivals, transport, infrastructure surveys (NITI Baseline Survey 2023).
Sector reports on tourism industry with state-level breakdowns, published quarterly.
Base monthly capacity derived from MoT ITS data anchored to 2019 (pre-COVID) peak. Figures represent mid-range estimates within published confidence intervals.
2020: 75% suppression; 2021: 45% suppression; 2022: 15% suppression applied uniformly, consistent with MoT emergency bulletins and UNWTO global recovery indices.
4% CAGR applied 2015–2019 per MoT historical trend. 2023–2025 recovery modelled at 105%–118% of 2019 baseline per IBEF recovery projections.
Spot-type specific monthly weights derived from seasonal visitor patterns published in MoT Monthly Reports and state tourism press releases.
UNESCO world heritage sites: 18–28% international. Major cities: 6–14%. Rural/lesser-known: 1–6%. Based on MoT Foreign Tourist Visits (FTV) report.
Age/gender distribution based on MoT Domestic Tourism Survey 2022 and UNWTO Youth Travel Barometer.
Spot-level annual totals cross-checked against MoT ITS state summary tables. Variance kept within ±8% of published figures for major spots.
Domestic + International = Monthly Total (ensured programmatically). Age-group sum = Monthly Total. No null or negative values.
Kumbh Mela 2019 (Prayagraj) special event spike modelled as 3x multiplier in Jan–Mar 2019 for Allahabad Fort / Prayagraj entries.
